title: Introduction to GIAS 2024 - The New Internal Audit Standards
description: Understand the major transition from IPPF to Global Internal Audit Standards effective January 2025

sections.title: ⚠️ CRITICAL EXAM UPDATE
sections.content: The **Global Internal Audit Standards (GIAS)** became effective **January 9, 2025**, replacing the previous International Professional Practices Framework (IPPF). This is the biggest change to internal audit standards in decades. Expect questions on both frameworks during the transition period!

sections.title: Why Did the Standards Change?
sections.content:
**The IIA undertook a multi-year project to modernize the standards:**

1. **Evolving Practice** - Internal audit has expanded beyond traditional assurance
2. **Stakeholder Expectations** - Boards and executives demand more value
3. **Technology Advances** - Digital transformation and data analytics
4. **Global Consistency** - One unified global framework
5. **Clarity** - Clearer requirements and implementation guidance
6. **Principles-Based** - More flexible application across contexts

sections.title: The Five Structural Domains of GIAS 2024
sections.content:
**GIAS 2024 organizes standards into FIVE overarching domains:**

**Domain I: PURPOSE OF INTERNAL AUDITING**
- Mission statement
- Mandate and accountability
- Independence and authority

**Domain II: ETHICS AND PROFESSIONALISM**
- Integrity, objectivity, confidentiality, competency
- Expectations for professional behavior

**Domain III: GOVERNING THE INTERNAL AUDIT FUNCTION**
- CAE role and responsibilities
- Board/audit committee relationship
- Quality assurance

**Domain IV: MANAGING THE INTERNAL AUDIT FUNCTION**
- Strategic planning
- Resource management
- Coordination with other providers

**Domain V: PERFORMING INTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES**
- Planning engagements
- Conducting work
- Communicating results
- Monitoring

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: GIAS Five Domains
sections.content:
**"PEGMP"** - Pronounce it "PEG-M-P"

• **P**urpose (Why we exist)
• **E**thics (How we behave)
• **G**overning (Who leads us)
• **M**anaging (How we operate)
• **P**erforming (What we do)

*Or: "Purpose Ethics Govern Manage Perform"*

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: GIAS 2024 became effective January 9, 2025
Replaces the multi-layer IPPF structure with unified framework
Five structural domains: Purpose, Ethics, Governing, Managing, Performing
15 thematic standards within the five domains
Principles integrated directly into standards (no separate Core Principles)
Exam may test BOTH old IPPF and new GIAS terminology during transition

knowledgeCheck.question: Which of the following is NOT a primary driver behind the IIA's decision to update the International Professional Practices Framework (IPPF) and create GIAS 2024?
options: The need to align with evolving internal audit practices that extend beyond traditional assurance.
Increased demands from stakeholders, such as boards and executives, for greater value from internal audit.
The desire to reduce the overall size and complexity of the existing IPPF.
The impact of digital transformation and advancements in data analytics on internal audit methodologies.
knowledgeCheck.explanation: The IIA aimed to modernize and enhance, not reduce, the IPPF. The driving forces were evolving practices, stakeholder expectations, and technological advancements.
knowledgeCheck.hint: Think about the pressures and changes impacting the internal audit profession.

reveal.question: Name the five structural domains of the GIAS 2024.
reveal.answer: 1. Purpose of Internal Auditing, 2. Ethics and Professionalism, 3. Governing the Internal Audit Function, 4. Managing the Internal Audit Function, 5. Performing Internal Audit Services

headers: Domain I: Purpose of Internal Auditing

rows.label: Primary Focus
values: Defines the mission, mandate, independence, and authority of internal auditing.
Establishes the ethical principles and professional conduct expected of internal auditors.

rows.label: Key Elements
values: Mission statement, Accountability to stakeholders, Organizational independence

rows.label: Impact
values: Shapes the strategic role and scope of the internal audit function.
Influences the trustworthiness and credibility of internal auditors and their work.

title: GIAS 2024: The 15 Standards Deep Dive
description: Detailed breakdown of all 15 Global Internal Audit Standards and their requirements

sections.title: Understanding the 15 Standards
sections.content:
**GIAS 2024 contains 15 individual Standards, organized within the 5 Domains:**

Unlike the old IPPF numbering (1000, 1100, 2000, etc.), GIAS uses a new thematic structure. Each Standard includes:
• **Principles** - High-level requirements
• **Standards** - Specific mandates
• **Topical Requirements** - Detailed guidance
• **Implementation Considerations** - Practical application tips

sections.title: Domain I: Purpose of Internal Auditing (Standards 1-3)
sections.content:
**Standard 1: Purpose of Internal Auditing**
• Defines the mission and value of internal auditing
• Establishes the mandate from the board
• Links to organizational governance

**Standard 2: Ethics and Professionalism**
• Replaces/incorporates the Code of Ethics
• Integrity, objectivity, confidentiality, competency
• Professional skepticism and judgment

**Standard 3: Governing the Internal Audit Function**
• CAE role and authority
• Board oversight and approval
• Quality assurance requirements

sections.title: Domain II: Independence (Standards 4-5)
sections.content:
**Standard 4: Independence and Objectivity**
• Organizational independence requirements
• Functional reporting to the board
• Threats and safeguards framework

**Standard 5: Competence**
• Knowledge, skills, abilities requirements
• Continuing professional development
• Collective competence of the function

sections.title: Domain III: Governing the Function (Standards 6-7)
sections.content:
**Standard 6: Roles and Responsibilities of the Board**
• Oversight of internal audit
• Charter approval
• CAE performance evaluation
• Communication expectations

**Standard 7: Chief Audit Executive Responsibilities**
• Strategic leadership
• Resource management
• Quality assurance program
• Stakeholder relationships

sections.title: Domain IV: Managing the Function (Standards 8-10)
sections.content:
**Standard 8: Strategic Planning**
• Risk-based audit planning
• Audit universe and priorities
• Long-term strategy alignment

**Standard 9: Resource Management**
• Staffing and competence
• Technology and tools
• Third-party resources

**Standard 10: Coordination and Reliance**
• Other assurance providers
• Combined assurance approach
• External auditor coordination

sections.title: Domain V: Performing Audit Services (Standards 11-15)
sections.content:
**Standard 11: Planning Engagements**
• Objectives, scope, criteria
• Risk assessment
• Work program development

**Standard 12: Conducting Engagements**
• Evidence gathering
• Analysis and evaluation
• Documentation requirements

**Standard 13: Forming Conclusions**
• Criteria application
• Findings development
• Root cause analysis

**Standard 14: Communicating Results**
• Report quality criteria
• Recommendations
• Dissemination

**Standard 15: Monitoring Actions**
• Follow-up process
• Risk acceptance
• Reporting to board on status

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: 15 Standards organized in 5 Domains
Standards 1-3: Purpose and Ethics
Standards 4-5: Independence and Competence
Standards 6-7: Governance (Board and CAE)
Standards 8-10: Managing the Function
Standards 11-15: Performing Audit Services
Each standard includes Principles, Requirements, and Considerations

knowledgeCheck.question: Under the GIAS 2024 structure, which of the following BEST describes the relationship between Principles and Standards?
options: Principles are specific mandates, while Standards are high-level requirements.
Principles and Standards are interchangeable terms within each domain.
Principles are high-level requirements that provide the foundation for the more specific Standards.
Standards are the overarching goals, and Principles provide the detailed steps for achieving them.
knowledgeCheck.explanation: The correct answer is C. Principles are designed as high-level requirements. Standards are more specific mandates derived from those principles. This reflects a top-down approach to internal audit governance.
knowledgeCheck.hint: Think about which is broader and which is more specific in scope.

reveal.question: Name the five Domains under the GIAS 2024.
reveal.answer: The five Domains are: I. Purpose of Internal Auditing, II. Independence, III. Proficiency and Due Professional Care, IV. Performance, and V. Governance.

rows.label: Mitigation
values: Functional reporting to the board; avoiding operational responsibilities
Disclosing conflicts of interest; careful planning and supervision of engagements

title: GIAS 2024: Mission and Principles Comparison
description: Compare the updated Mission statement and how Principles are now integrated

sections.title: The Mission of Internal Audit - Then and Now
sections.content:
**IPPF Mission (Pre-2025):**
"To enhance and protect organizational value by providing risk-based and objective assurance, advice, and insight."

**GIAS 2024 Mission (Current):**
"To enhance and protect organizational value by providing risk-based and objective assurance, advice, and insight."

✅ **The Mission statement remains essentially UNCHANGED** - This is the one constant through the transition!

sections.title: What Happened to the 10 Core Principles?
sections.content:
**In the old IPPF, there were 10 separate Core Principles:**
1. Demonstrates integrity
2. Demonstrates competence and due professional care
3. Is objective and free from undue influence
4. Aligns with strategies, objectives, and risks
5. Is appropriately positioned and adequately resourced
6. Demonstrates quality and continuous improvement
7. Communicates effectively
8. Provides risk-based assurance
9. Is insightful, proactive, and future-focused
10. Promotes organizational improvement

**In GIAS 2024, these principles are INTEGRATED into the 15 Standards** - they're no longer a separate component. For example:
- Principle 1 (Integrity) → Standard 2 (Ethics)
- Principle 5 (Resourced) → Standard 9 (Resource Management)
- Principle 8 (Risk-based) → Standard 8 (Strategic Planning)

sections.title: ⚠️ Exam Trap
sections.content:
Questions may still ask about the **10 Core Principles** since:
1. Many organizations are still transitioning
2. The principles are reflected in GIAS (just differently organized)
3. The concepts remain valid even if structure changed

**Know BOTH the old 10 Principles AND how they map to GIAS 2024!**

sections.title: Code of Ethics Integration
sections.content:
**Old Structure:**
Code of Ethics was a separate mandatory element with:
- 4 Principles (Integrity, Objectivity, Confidentiality, Competency)
- Rules of Conduct for each principle

**GIAS 2024:**
- Standard 2 (Ethics and Professionalism) incorporates ethics requirements
- The four foundational principles remain but are now within the standards framework
- Behavioral expectations are embedded throughout

**The IOCO principles (Integrity, Objectivity, Confidentiality, Competency) remain critical** - just organized differently!

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: Mission statement is essentially UNCHANGED
10 Core Principles are now INTEGRATED into the 15 Standards
Code of Ethics is embedded in Standard 2 (Ethics and Professionalism)
The concepts remain - only the organization changed
Still memorize the 10 original principles for exam transition period
IOCO (Integrity, Objectivity, Confidentiality, Competency) still foundational

knowledgeCheck.question: Under the GIAS 2024, which of the following BEST describes the core purpose of internal audit?
options: To identify and mitigate all risks within the organization.
To enhance and protect organizational value by providing risk-based and objective assurance, advice, and insight.
To ensure compliance with all applicable laws and regulations.
To improve the efficiency and effectiveness of operational processes.
knowledgeCheck.explanation: The GIAS 2024 mission statement explicitly focuses on enhancing and protecting organizational value through risk-based and objective assurance, advice, and insight. While the other options represent important aspects of internal auditing, they do not encompass the entire mission.
knowledgeCheck.hint: Consider the keywords in the mission statement: 'enhance,' 'protect,' 'risk-based,' 'objective,' 'assurance,' 'advice,' and 'insight.'

reveal.question: What are the four foundational principles of the Code of Ethics that are now integrated within Standard 2 (Ethics and Professionalism) of the GIAS 2024?
reveal.answer: The four foundational principles are: Integrity, Objectivity, Confidentiality, and Competency.

rows.label: Code of Ethics
values: Separate mandatory element with principles and rules of conduct.
Integrated into Standard 2 (Ethics and Professionalism); foundational principles remain.

title: GIAS 2024: Transition Guidance for the CIA Exam
description: Practical guidance on how to approach GIAS 2024 vs IPPF questions on the CIA exam

sections.title: How the Exam Handles the Transition
sections.content:
**The IIA has announced:**

1. **Transition Period**: Questions will reflect BOTH frameworks
2. **No Penalty**: You won't be penalized for using old terminology
3. **Substance Over Form**: The concepts matter more than labels
4. **Gradual Shift**: Expect increasing GIAS emphasis over time

**What this means for you:**
- Know IPPF terminology (likely still appears)
- Know GIAS 2024 structure (new questions will use this)
- Focus on underlying CONCEPTS (these haven't changed)

sections.title: Quick Reference: Old vs New Terminology
Exam Strategy Tips
sections.content:
**When you see a CIA exam question:**

**1. DON'T PANIC** if you see old IPPF terminology
- The concepts are the same
- Answer based on the underlying principle

**2. READ CAREFULLY** for GIAS-specific language
- "The Standards require..." could refer to either
- Context clues will indicate which framework

**3. FOCUS ON CONCEPTS**
- Independence → CAE reports to board
- Due care → Reasonable care, not infallibility
- Quality → Internal + External assessments

**4. WHEN UNSURE**
- GIAS 2024 is more principles-based
- IPPF was more rules-based
- If asked about specific standard numbers (1110, 2010), use IPPF logic

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: Exam accepts both IPPF and GIAS 2024 terminology during transition
Focus on concepts - they haven't changed materially
Know the crosswalk between old standard numbers and new domains
GIAS 2024 is more principles-based and integrated
When in doubt, apply the underlying professional principle

knowledgeCheck.question: An internal auditor encounters a CIA exam question using terminology from the old IPPF that they are familiar with. According to the IIA's transition guidance, what is the MOST appropriate course of action?
options: Immediately skip the question, as the terminology is outdated.
Answer the question based on the underlying principle of the concept, even if the terminology is unfamiliar.
Attempt to translate the old terminology into GIAS terminology and then answer the question.
Choose the answer option that contains the most complex and technical language, assuming it is the most up-to-date.
knowledgeCheck.explanation: The IIA has stated that you won't be penalized for using old terminology and that the substance of the concepts matters more than the labels. Therefore, answering based on the underlying principle is the best approach.
knowledgeCheck.hint: Think about the IIA's guidance on 'substance over form'.

reveal.question: What are the four key points that the IIA has announced regarding the GIAS transition on the CIA exam?
reveal.answer:
1. Transition Period: Questions will reflect BOTH frameworks.
2. No Penalty: You won't be penalized for using old terminology.
3. Substance Over Form: The concepts matter more than labels.
4. Gradual Shift: Expect increasing GIAS emphasis over time.

knowledgeCheck.question: Which of the following BEST describes the expected trend in GIAS emphasis on the CIA exam over time?
options: GIAS will be immediately and fully implemented, with no trace of the old IPPF.
The old IPPF will continue to be the primary focus of the exam for the foreseeable future.
GIAS emphasis will gradually increase, with the old IPPF eventually being phased out.
GIAS and the old IPPF will be given equal weight indefinitely.
knowledgeCheck.explanation: The IIA has indicated a gradual shift towards GIAS, meaning the emphasis will increase over time as the old IPPF is phased out.
knowledgeCheck.hint: Consider the IIA's statement about a 'Gradual Shift'.
